anova.desc: Compares the means of 3+ groups. With Tukey HSD post-hoc when significant.
anova.label: One-way ANOVA

chi_square.desc: Whether two categorical variables are related. Cramér's V as the effect size.
chi_square.label: Chi² test of independence

correlation.desc: Pearson for linear continuous variables, Spearman for ranked/monotonic ones.
correlation.label: Correlation

correlation_matrix.desc: Pearson r between every pair of the selected continuous variables.
correlation_matrix.label: Correlation matrix

cronbach_alpha.desc: Internal consistency of a multi-item scale (e.g. several Likert questions).
cronbach_alpha.label: Cronbach's alpha

descriptives.desc: Mean, median, SD and quartiles for a single continuous variable.
descriptives.label: Descriptive statistics

analyses.disclaimer_short: ⚠ Result intended for quick exploration. Verify in R / SPSS / JASP before publication.
analyses.empty_hint: Pick your variables and click Run analysis.
analyses.export_note_1: ⚠ These statistics are meant for quick exploration. For publication, export the data (xlsx/CSV)
analyses.export_note_2: and run the analysis in R, SPSS, JASP or Python (scipy/statsmodels).

regression.desc: Simple OLS: fits Y = a + b·X, reports R² and the significance of the slope.
regression.label: Linear regression Y ~ X

t_test.desc: Compares the means of 2 groups. Welch for independent samples, standard for paired ones.
t_test.label: t-test (Welch)

correlation_heatmap.desc: An N×N correlation matrix with colours.
correlation_heatmap.label: Correlation heatmap

histogram.desc: Distribution of a single continuous variable.

scatter.desc: X-Y points for two continuous variables + Pearson r.

text_responses.desc: A list of open-ended responses with a group filter.
text_responses.label: Text responses

common.pick_study_first: Select a study from the list above.

dashboard.default_layout_hint: Default layout generated from the study schema. Edit to customize it.
dashboard.et_calib_failed_hint: The participant gave consent, but calibration failed
dashboard.no_widgets: No widgets. Click ✏️ Edit → + Add widget.
dashboard.note_placeholder: e.g. “Spike after pre-registration”, “Check this difference in ANOVA”…
dashboard.note_visibility_hint: The note will appear under the widget — only you (and people with the read-only link) will see it.
dashboard.profile_delete_prompt: Which profile should be deleted? Available: {{names}}
dashboard.reset_confirm: Clear your configuration and return to the default layout? Your changes will be undone.
dashboard.reset_hint: Clear the configuration and return to the default
dashboard.share_copy_hint: Press OK to copy it to the clipboard.
dashboard.share_days_prompt: How many days should the link be valid for? (1-365, default 30)
dashboard.widget_delete_confirm: Delete widget “{{title}}”? (You can add it again from “+ Add widget”.)
dashboard.wiz_title_ph: e.g. Mean session time by condition

demog.bounds_hint_edit: Empty field = no limit. For the Text type the values are the character count (min/maxlength). For the Number type it is the allowed value range (e.g. 18–120 for age).
demog.bounds_hint_new: Empty field = no limit. For the Text type the values are the character count. For the Number type it is the allowed value range (e.g. 18–120 for age).
demog.label_ph: e.g. What is your employment status?
demog.multiselect_hint_1: For the Multiselect type the participant ticks any number of checkboxes —
demog.multiselect_hint_2: in the export they are saved as a comma-separated list in a single cell.
demog.no_slug: Study slug not found — refresh the list.

export.builder_toggle_hint: — hide, rename, reorder. Changes never delete data from the database.
export.confirm_del_preview:
Delete ALL preview sessions (along with their reactions, ratings and answers) for this study?

Production participant sessions will remain untouched. This operation cannot be undone.
export.csv_title: Dane_surowe sheet only — quick import into R/SPSS/Python
export.del_preview_title: Deletes both completed ({{done}}) and unfinished ({{incomplete}}) preview sessions
export.delete_profile_prompt: Which profile do you want to delete? Available: {{names}}
export.eb_bulk_hide_title: Set visible=false for the selected columns
export.eb_bulk_show_title: Set visible=true for the selected columns
export.eb_hidden_manual: ({{n}} manually, the rest in collapsed groups)
export.eb_hint: ↔ Drag a column header to reorder · ⋮ column menu to rename or hide
export.eb_no_rows_hint: (no data — check that you have completed sessions, or enable "Include preview sessions")
export.eb_save_profile_title: Save the current configuration as a new profile
export.eb_show_hidden_title: Whether hidden/collapsed columns appear in this preview (the export excludes them either way)
export.layout_saved: Column layout saved. The next export will use it.
export.no_preview_sessions: No completed preview sessions to include.
export.preview_incomplete_title: Preview sessions that were never finished — they will not appear in the export even with "Include preview sessions" enabled
export.profile_loaded: Profile "{{name}}" loaded. Click "Save layout" to make it permanent.
export.profile_no_sheet_config: This profile has no configuration for this sheet.
export.reset_confirm: Reset the layout to default? Your changes (order, hidden columns, header names) will be reverted.
export.reset_done: Reset — click "Save layout" to make it permanent.

header.db_backup_title: Download a full copy of the database (.db) — administrator only
header.mode_title: How your own sessions in this study are counted

konfig.comments_help: Master switch for the comment field under a post. When on, the participant can type text under every post whose post editor has "💬 Comment field" checked (Phase 2). Without this toggle the per-post show_comment stays inactive (you can still set it in the admin UI, but the participant sees nothing). Comments go to the export in the post_N_comment column.
konfig.completion_delay_help_1: How many seconds after the debriefing screen the automatic redirect fires.
konfig.completion_delay_help_3: the agency wants to control the final screen itself). Active only when a completion link is set.
konfig.completion_notice_help_1: If you fill this in, a box pinned to the top appears on the debriefing screen
konfig.completion_notice_help_2: with this text and a "Back to the panel →" button (clicking = an immediate redirect,
konfig.completion_notice_help_3: without waiting for the timer). The box stays visible while the participant scrolls through the debriefing text.
konfig.completion_notice_help_4: Empty = no box (only a small message at the bottom of the page, as before).
konfig.completion_notice_help_5: Active only when a completion link is set.
konfig.completion_notice_label: Notice text in the sticky box above the debriefing
konfig.completion_notice_ph: e.g. After reading the debriefing you will return to the Agency X panel — your credit will be granted automatically.
konfig.completion_url_help_1: After finishing the study the participant is redirected to this URL. Placeholders:
konfig.completion_url_help_2: {ext_id} (the ID from the URL parameter) and {session_id} (our session token).
konfig.completion_url_help_3: Empty = no redirect, standard final screen.
konfig.consent_ph: Participant consent text... (leave empty to fill in later)
konfig.custom_domain_help_1: If you have set up a CNAME record for a subdomain (e.g. study.example.org
konfig.custom_domain_help_2: → this app's address), enter EXACTLY that host name here — without https://,
konfig.custom_domain_help_3: without a trailing /. The participant will then open
konfig.custom_domain_help_5: this specific study. The admin panel and other studies will be unavailable on that host.
konfig.custom_domain_help_6: Empty = the study is available only via
konfig.custom_domain_help_7: on the app's main address (as it is now).
konfig.debrief_ph: Debriefing text shown after the study ends...
konfig.debrief_preview_title: Opens the debriefing screen in a new tab, without going through the whole study
konfig.debrief_show_posts: Show the list of posts marked true/false
konfig.decline_delay_help_1: Default 4. 0 = immediate redirect. Active only when a decline
konfig.decline_immediate_help_1: When checked, clicking "I do not consent" runs
konfig.decline_immediate_help_3: the participant does not even see the local "We understand your decision" screen.
konfig.decline_immediate_help_4: The delay and the sticky box are then ignored. Active only when a
konfig.decline_immediate_help_5: decline link is set. Useful when the agency panel has its own
konfig.decline_immediate_label: Skip the decline screen — redirect immediately
konfig.decline_notice_help_1: The same field as for the completion link, only for the decline screen.
konfig.decline_notice_help_2: Filled in = a sticky box with a "Back to the panel →" button. Empty = a small message at the bottom.
konfig.decline_notice_label: Notice text in the sticky box on the decline screen
konfig.decline_notice_ph: e.g. You will shortly be redirected back to the Agency X panel.
konfig.decline_url_help_1: The URL you give the agency as "screen-out" / "dropout" — used when the participant clicks
konfig.decline_url_help_2: "I do not consent" on the consent screen. A panel normally expects a different URL than for
konfig.decline_url_help_3: completion (because no credit is granted). The same placeholders as for the completion link:
konfig.decline_url_help_4: {ext_id}, {session_id}. Empty = the participant stays on the local
konfig.decline_url_label: Consent-decline link (when a participant does not agree to the study)
konfig.demographics_help: Demographic questions (age, place of residence, education, gender + any questions from the Demographic q. tab). At the end is often preferred when the researcher worries that demographics could prime the participant's behaviour in the main task. Skip for recruitment from platforms that supply demographics out-of-band (Prolific, Lucid). You can change the screen's heading and subtitle in ⚙ → Interface translations (keys demographics.title, demographics.subtitle) — changes go to the database and survive a redeploy.
konfig.desc_label: Description (visible only in the admin panel)
konfig.empty_builder_only: The configurator is available for studies in builder mode. This study uses the classic settings (Settings tab).
konfig.empty_pick_study: Select a study from the list above to open the configurator.
konfig.ext_param_help_1: The agency appends something like ?res_id=ABC123 to the link. The value
konfig.ext_param_help_2: goes to the external_id column in the export. Default: res_id.
konfig.ext_param_label: Name of the URL parameter carrying the respondent ID
konfig.instruction_show_icons: Show a preview of the reaction icons (👍 / 👎 / 🔄 / 🚩)
konfig.logic_empty: No rules yet. Add your own or use a template:
konfig.logic_f_message_ph: e.g. Thank you, this study is for people 18+.
konfig.logic_help: "If → then" rules: skip a part of the study or end it early based on a demographic answer, an answer to a post question, or the assigned condition (Manipulation). Rules do not fire in the preview. Methodological note: ending a study based on a participant's answer can affect your sample — use it deliberately.
konfig.logic_reaction_hint: For reactions use the contains operator with the values: like, dislike, share or flag (e.g. "contains flag" = the participant flagged some post).
konfig.logic_timing_opt_interaction: After a reaction to a post
konfig.logic_tpl_age_message: Thank you, but this study is only available to adults (18+).
konfig.logic_tpl_skip: Template: skip a part in condition A
konfig.manip_help: Optional between-subjects split into two conditions (A and B). Every post has an A and a B variant (you set them in the Posts tab); the participant randomly sees one of them. This is a single binary split — for more factors use metric conditions (2×2 design).
konfig.manip_info_1: ℹ️ Each participant is assigned at random to condition A or B (between-subjects split).
konfig.manip_info_2: Condition A shows variant A of every post, condition B — variant B.
konfig.manip_info_3: You set the content of both variants on each post in the Posts tab.
konfig.manip_info_4: For a 2×2 design, combine this with the "Metric conditions (social proof)" section below.
konfig.manip_remove_title: Remove the split — every participant will see variant A
konfig.metrics_help: Second between-subjects factor: metric ranges (likes / shares / reactions) randomised per condition — e.g. high vs low social proof. Each participant is randomly assigned to one metric condition; combining this with the A/B split above gives you a 2×2 design. min/max = the range the numbers are drawn from; if you enter a specific base value on a post, it takes priority over the range.
konfig.metrics_show: Show metric counters to participants (👍 likes, shares, reactions under posts)
konfig.multi_reactions_help: By default reactions work like radio buttons — clicking a new one cancels the previous one. Once enabled, the participant can like, share and flag the same post at the same time (this stacks with a comment too). Like and dislike stay mutually exclusive. A second click on the same reaction deselects it. Every click goes to the database — in the export, undone reactions have is_undo=1, so the researcher sees the full path.
konfig.no_consent_ph: Message shown when a participant declines to take part...
konfig.panel_help_1: Configuration for studies recruited through an agency. Both fields are optional —
konfig.part_max_time_hint: seconds (0 = no limit). When the time is up the participant moves on to the next part automatically, regardless of the state of the requirements.
konfig.part_req_title_hint: (structural — they win over "Interaction with a post required")
konfig.part_show_reactions_hint: — turn off to hide interactions in this part
konfig.part_show_transition: Show a transition screen before this part
konfig.part_transition_emoji_hint: — leave empty to hide the emoji.
konfig.parts_help: Each part has its own post layout. You can add several parts with transitions between them.
konfig.q_likert_desc_ph: Extra description shown under the question text...
konfig.q_likert_start_title: The first button of the scale — 1 (the classic option) or 0 (when the study models a 0–N scale). Changing this value after data collection has started CHANGES the meaning of earlier answers — use it deliberately, for new questions.
konfig.q_screen_subtitle_ph: e.g. Your answers will help us understand…
konfig.q_screen_title_ph: e.g. A few questions about this post
konfig.req_count_title: 0 = optional (the participant sees ∞ — they can do as many as they like, it does not block Next)
konfig.req_count_title_short: 0 = optional (the participant sees ∞ — it does not block Next)
konfig.req_help: Empty list → no structural requirements (back-compat: only "Interaction with a post required" counts). "⭐ Any interaction" counts every reaction / comment together.
konfig.save_demo_added: Added an example post for the preview. Replace it with your own content in the post editor.
konfig.save_demo_content: Example post content. Add your own posts in the post editor.
konfig.save_lang_not_pl: Select a language other than Polish.
konfig.screens_help: Consent and debriefing are always shown. The rest are optional.

konta.assign_modal_help: Select the studies this researcher should have full edit access to (posts, configuration, dashboard, export). They see only the studies assigned to them; they cannot delete them or manage access. "Owner" = the study belongs to this account.
konta.col_studies_tooltip: Number of studies owned by the account
konta.confirm_role: Change the role of account "{{name}}" to {{role}}?
konta.del_self_disabled: You cannot delete your own account
konta.del_warn_owner: WARNING: this account owns {{n}} studies — after deletion you will take them over as administrator.
konta.err_password_short: The password must be at least 8 characters long.
konta.f_password_hint: (min. 8 characters — pass it on to the user)
konta.intro_admin: Accounts are created by an administrator (no self-registration). Each researcher sees only their own studies; the administrator sees all of them.
konta.intro_researcher: Assign other researchers to studies you own. "🔗 Studies" opens a list of your studies — select the ones a given person should have full edit access to.
konta.mode_preview_toast: Your sessions on study links will be marked as preview (hidden from the dashboard/export).
konta.mode_production_toast: ⚠ Your sessions on study links will count as PRODUCTION. Remember to switch back to Preview mode after testing.
konta.no_studies_to_share: You have no studies to share.

panes.demog_preview_title: Opens the demographics screen in a new tab, skipping consent and instructions
panes.export_screens_title: Open every study screen (condition A) as a report appendix — save it as a PDF
panes.konfig_empty: Select a study from the list above to open the configurator.
panes.posts_library_title: Copy a ready-made post from the reusable library
panes.settings_empty: Select a study from the list above to edit its settings.

posts.avatar_delete_confirm: Delete the avatar? It will fall back to initials.
posts.avatar_hint: Click the circle to choose a file (jpg/png/webp, max 5 MB)
posts.comments_auto_enabled: Comments were also enabled at the study level (required by the per-post toggle).
posts.comments_count_title: Updates automatically from the comment list below
posts.delete_confirm:
Delete post "{{title}}"?
This will also delete the related reactions and ratings. It cannot be undone.
posts.headline: Headline
posts.lib_copied: Post copied from the library into the study.
posts.lib_delete_confirm: Delete "{{name}}" from the library? Posts already copied into studies remain untouched.
posts.lib_empty: The library is empty. Use "🗂 To library" on any post to add the first template.
posts.lib_hint: Click "➕ Add to study" to copy the template into the current study. The copy is independent — later changes to the template do not affect it.
posts.no_posts: No posts yet. Click "+ Add post" to add the first one.
posts.parts_hint: (tick every part this post should appear in)
posts.parts_none_hint: Nothing ticked = the post goes to the first part (backward compatibility).
posts.show_avatar: Show the avatar on this post (turn off for an anonymous source)
posts.to_library_title: Save this post to the library for reuse
posts.visible_interactions_hint: (per-post; also limited by the study and part settings)

settings.clarity_enable: Enable MS Clarity for this study
settings.clarity_no_pid: Enabled, but no Project ID — enter it above
settings.clarity_pid_hint: Create a separate project in the MS Clarity panel for this study and paste its Project ID
settings.clarity_pid_hint2: (you will find it in the Clarity dashboard URL).
settings.enable_comments: Enable the comment field under the post
settings.exp_conditions_hint: Each participant is randomly assigned to one of the active conditions. Use the 💬 toggle to show the debunking comment only in selected conditions.
settings.ext_param_label: Name of the URL parameter carrying the respondent ID
settings.eyetracking_hint1: Participants will be asked to consent to camera use (they can decline with no effect on the study).
settings.eyetracking_hint2: The camera image is never stored or transmitted — only gaze coordinates.
settings.eyetracking_hint3: The data goes to the Eye_tracking sheet in the Excel export and the gaze CSV.
settings.institution_hint: (optional — appears as a badge above the title)
settings.panel_hint1: Configure this if an agency recruits participants and passes the respondent ID via the link.
settings.panel_hint2: Both fields are optional — empty = standard flow without integration.
settings.participant_title_hint: Shown on the consent screen. If empty — the admin name is displayed.
settings.show_debunk_title: Show the debunking comment to participants in this condition
settings.show_metrics: Show social interactions (number of likes, shares, etc.)
settings.show_reactions: Show reaction buttons (like / dislike / share / flag)
settings.style_conditions_hint: Each participant is randomly assigned to one of the active conditions — corresponding to content A or B in the posts.
settings.title: Study settings

studies.delete_confirm:
Delete the study "{{name}}"?

This cannot be undone — all data (sessions, reactions, ratings) will be permanently deleted.
studies.duplicate_confirm: Duplicate this study? Settings and posts will be copied (without session data).
studies.empty: No studies yet. Create your first study.

translate.pick_non_polish: Select a language other than Polish.
translate.saved_editable: Translation saved! You can now edit individual fields.

utils.badge_db_title: Edited in the database {{date}} — survives redeploy
utils.badge_file_title: From the locale file (baseline) — your edit will be saved to the database
utils.saved_no_refresh: Saved, but could not refresh — check manually
utils.saved_translations: Saved translations for {{lang}} (stored in the database)
utils.translations_hint: Edit the participant interface labels. The 🇵🇱 tab lets you edit the Polish wording.
